data.narrative:
Skydio, a Series E autonomous systems company in Defense & Security, last posted at $2.2B post-money on $230M total raised. The applicable comp universe comprises 10 peers spanning public and private markets: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private firms including Flock Safety, Anduril Industries, and Saronic at comparable or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.4x Series E step-up multiplier applied to the $2.2B last post-money, implies $3.1B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comps—Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Flock Safety ($7.5B Series E), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), and Epirus ($1.4B Series D)—yields a median valuation of $6.0B, with a range of $1.4B to $9.2B.

The blended analysis supports a valuation range of $2.6B to $6.9B, with a median of $4.5B. This 2.6x spread reflects material dispersion within the peer set, particularly driven by the $9.2B outlier (Saronic) and the $1.4B floor (Epirus). Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing event, revenue traction relative to defense-sector peers, and regulatory tailwinds specific to autonomous systems in national security applications. The peer-median method carries higher weight given stage alignment, though the wide comp range underscores valuation sensitivity to company-specific execution metrics not captured in cross-sectional analysis.
